meta.title: Convert MKV to MP3 — Free, In Your Browser, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Extract the audio from a .mkv video to an .mp3 file right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Convert MKV to MP3 — Free, No Upload
og.description: Pull the audio out of a .mkv (Matroska) video into .mp3, locally in your browser.
hero.title: Convert MKV to MP3 — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop a .mkv video and pull out just the audio as an .mp3 — the music, the dialogue, the soundtrack. It's extracted on your own device, so the file is never uploaded.
prose.p1: MKV (Matroska) is the container of choice for films, episodes and high-quality downloads — flexible, but big, and not every player handles it. When you only want the sound, extracting to MP3 leaves a small file that plays in any music app, on any phone or in the car, with no need to carry the whole video around.
prose.p2: MKV files are often very large, so the upload-to-a-server converters are slow and hand over your footage. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't upload anything — the audio is extracted locally in your browser, so even a multi-gigabyte .mkv starts instantly and stays private.
faq.a1: No. The extraction happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — open your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.q2: My MKV has several audio tracks — which one?
faq.a2: The primary (default) audio track is extracted. If your file has multiple languages or commentary tracks, the one marked default in the container is the one you'll get.
faq.q3: Can it handle a large movie file?
faq.a3: Yes. Audio is tiny next to video, so even a full-length .mkv extracts quickly and the resulting MP3 stays small — and because nothing uploads, file size isn't limited by a server.
